hacking.notes: Attacker gained unauthorized access to RXNT's cloud-based EHR/e-prescribing solution and obtained a copy of stored patient data. Entry method/variety not disclosed by RXNT.

confidentiality.notes: Names, dates of birth, contact information, patient IDs, prescription/medical information, and Social Security numbers. Attacker obtained a copy of data stored within the RXNT system.

plus.analyst_notes:
Closes https://github.com/vz-risk/VCDB/issues/23381
Encoded by AI — encode-veris-incident skill version 20260702T132320Z

PROVIDER/SUPPLY-CHAIN BREACH — coded from the provider's perspective per the skill's third-party rule. RXNT (Networking Technology Inc), a cloud-based e-prescribing / practice-management / EHR SaaS vendor, was the breached entity; the compromise reached patient data belonging to MANY of its healthcare customers across multiple states (reported impact tens of thousands of individuals: ~49,338 TX, 11,442 SC, 4,480 WA, 3,634 NH, 1,163 MA, 5 VT residents, plus prescription data for members of Congress via the Office of the Attending Physician). Because a single shared vendor system common to its clientele was the point of compromise and multiple client orgs were hit, RXNT is coded as the PRIMARY victim and the GitHub-issue org (First Sight Family Vision, formerly Jason R Egbert OD PC — the Battle Ground, WA optometry practice, 1,225 of whose patients were affected) is listed under victim.secondary. One incident coded, not one per customer, to avoid inflating counts.

Actor: external, variety Unknown and entry method/variety undisclosed by RXNT (matches the issue's 'Hacking-ext-unknown' template tag). Motive inferred as Financial from the theft of a large volume of SSN/PII/medical data. action.hacking.vector coded 'Partner' per the skill's provider-breach convention to capture that impact propagated through the provider relationship; the actual technical entry vector into RXNT was not disclosed (noted here rather than guessed).

data_disclosure = Yes: RXNT's investigation confirmed the attacker accessed systems and obtained a copy of stored data. confidentiality.state = Stored (at-rest; encryption status not stated). data_total OMITTED: only a partial, per-jurisdiction/per-customer count is available (e.g. 1,225 for the issue's practice; per-state totals) and no single defensible cross-victim total is published, so logging one number would mislead.

Discovery: internal — RXNT itself identified the unauthorized access on 2026-03-03 (method unspecified → Unknown). Timeline: unauthorized access confirmed 2026-03-01 to 2026-03-03 (incident begin = 2026-03-01); discovered 2026-03-03 (Days: 2). plus.dbir_year = 2027 (incident began March 2026, within the Jan–Oct window → year+1).

SOURCES: (1) issue-linked HIPAA Journal article on First Sight Family Vision / Jason R Egbert OD PC; (2) NEWER independent source found via web search — Security Boulevard (May 2026) 'RXNT Healthcare Software Breach Exposes Patient Data Across Multiple Provider Clients', which corroborated and EXPANDED the picture: it revealed the far larger multi-state, multi-customer scope (tens of thousands of individuals, Congress OAP) that the single-practice issue did not, reinforcing the provider-as-primary-victim decision; no facts from the earlier source were contradicted. (3) HIPAA Journal RXNT-focused article confirming exfiltration ('obtained a copy of the data'). No named threat actor in any source; social-media links were not cited (followed to underlying articles).

UNKNOWN/INFERRED FIELDS: actor.external.variety, .name, .country (all Unknown); action.hacking.variety and true entry vector (undisclosed); discovery_method.internal.variety (Unknown); actor motive (inferred Financial); data_total (omitted).

Reviewed by swidup 2026-07-10: changed event_chain step 1 attribute from ia to unk since the entry method — and therefore the attribute — is undisclosed.

event_chain.summary: Unauthorized access to RXNT cloud EHR system (entry method undisclosed)
Copy of stored patient data obtained (exfiltration)

summary: Networking Technology Inc, doing business as RXNT — a cloud-based electronic prescribing, practice-management, and EHR SaaS vendor — suffered a hacking breach in which an external actor gained unauthorized access to its systems between March 1 and March 3, 2026 and obtained a copy of stored patient data belonging to many of its healthcare customers. Compromised data included names, dates of birth, contact information, patient IDs, prescription/medical information, and Social Security numbers, affecting tens of thousands of individuals across multiple states. Among the affected customer practices was First Sight Family Vision (formerly Jason R Egbert OD PC) of Battle Ground, Washington, 1,225 of whose patients were reported to HHS OCR.

victim_id: First Sight Family Vision (Jason R Egbert OD PC); 621111
